claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex!

To continue assessing your eligibility, I have a few more questions:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you!

**Are you and Alex both living in the United Kingdom (England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that.

**Is Alex in full-time education or training?** (At 13, I'd expect Alex is still at school, but I just want to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is still at school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming.

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This means you're the one paying for things like food, clothes, and a place for Alex to live.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely - I look after Alex and pay for everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's helpful.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is there anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I have one final question:

**What is your immigration status?** For example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have another type of visa or immigration status?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information! I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- Alex is 13 years old and lives with you
- Alex is in full-time education (school)
- You are responsible for Alex (paying for their food, clothes, and accommodation)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Alex is under 16** – at 13 years old, Alex meets the age requirement
✅ **You live in the UK** – you're both living in Manchester
✅ **You're responsible for Alex** – you live with Alex and pay for their needs (food, clothes, accommodation)
✅ **You're a British citizen** – this satisfies the immigration/residency requirements
✅ **No one else is claiming** – only one person can claim Child Benefit per child

You can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. If you have any other questions about the application process or anything else, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is under 16 years old (13), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for Alex by paying for their food, clothes, and accommodation. The claimant is a British citizen, which satisfies the residency/immigration requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 13-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex and meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. As the child is under 16 and in full-time education, and no one else is claiming for them, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is under 16 years old (13), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for Alex by paying for their food, clothes, and accommodation. The claimant is a British citizen, which satisfies the residency/immigration requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 13-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex and meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. As the child is under 16 and in full-time education, and no one else is claiming for them, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit.
